free_text: Measurements of proton induced gamma-ray emission
cross-sections on Mg from 1.0 to 3.0 MeV
HPGe detector, placed at an angle of 90 degree
to the beam axis and at a distance of 5.19 cm from
simultaneously by an ion implanted Si detector (25
mm2 area, 300 lm thickness and 13 keV energy
resolution) placed at the scattering angle of 165
degree, 183 mm distant from the target.
Natural Mg (60 ug/cm2) evaporated on a thin self-
calibration was estimated to be about 2 keV.
The total uncertainties were obtained from
the following partial uncertainties: The systematic
Statistical unc. of the photo peak counts 1-4% at
proton energies above 2.24 MeV and 26% at proton
energies below 2.24 MeV. Unc. of the Ag proton
scattering cross section considered as negligible
and the statistical unc. of the proton peak area less
